messages.content:
Simple present

Complete the article about Nathan Wolfe with the simple present form of the verbs in parentheses.
How one scientist fights for global health

Nathan Wolfe is a scientist and he 
 (work) all over the world. He 
 (specialize) in viruses and diseases, and he often 
 (go) to places with health problems. In particular, he 
 (study) viruses and diseases from animals. It’s an important job because he 
 (want) to know how these viruses spread from animals to humans. As a result, Nathan 
 (spend) a lot of time in regions with wildlife.

In the modern world, humans 
 (not / stay) in one place anymore, so new viruses also 
 (travel) more easily. When humans 
 (visit) different regions, they 
do not realize
 (not / realize) how easy it is to bring a new kind of disease back with them.

However, the modern world with its technology also 
help
 (help) Nathan with his work. He often works in parts of the world where people 
 (not / have) electricity. A cell phone allows Nathan to continue his life-saving work.

Nathan Wolfe is a scientist and he works all over the world. He specializes in viruses and diseases, and he often goes to places with health problems. In particular, he studies viruses and diseases from animals. It’s an important job because he wants to know how these viruses spread from animals to humans. As a result, Nathan spends a lot of time in regions with wildlife.

In the modern world, humans do not stay in one place anymore, so new viruses also travel more easily. When humans visit different regions, they do not realize how easy it is to bring a new kind of disease back with them.

However, the modern world with its technology also helps Nathan with his work. He often works in parts of the world where people do not have electricity. A cell phone allows Nathan to continue his life-saving work.

Write questions about Nathan Wolfe and his work. Use the simple present.
 (where / Nathan / work)
(where / he / often / go)
(what / he / study)
. (where / he / spend / a lot of time)
(why / new viruses / travel more easily)
(people / have electricity / in every part of the world) 
 (how / Nathan / communicate)

Where does Nathan Wolfe work?
Where does he often go?
What does he study?
Where does he spend a lot of time?
Why do new viruses travel more easily?
Do people have electricity in every part of the world?
How does Nathan communicate?

Find and correct the mistakes by rewriting the following sentences. When you are finished, click on the Feedback button to see suggested answers.
1. I often am tired at work.
2. We twice a week eat out in a restaurant.

1. I am often tired at work.
2. We eat out in a restaurant twice a week.

Adverbs of frequency

Find and correct the mistakes by rewriting the following sentences. When you are finished, click on the Feedback button to see suggested answers.
3. I have two or three times a day a cup of coffee.
4. They don’t play often board games.

3. I have a cup of coffee two or three times a day.
4. They don’t often play board games.
5. Does usually she take public transportation?
Does she usually take public transportation?

Present continuous

Look at the spell check box. Then write the -ing form of these verbs.
 live 
living

2. drop 
dropping

3. swim 
swiming

4. have 

5. lie 

6. take 

7. get 

8. jog

4. having
5. lying
6. taking
7. getting
8. jogging

Mobile medicine

Sarubai Salve goes to work twice a day. She leaves her home once at 9:00 a.m. and then again at 6:00 p.m. to visit people in her village of Jawalke. The village has about 240 families and, with another woman named Babai Sathe, Sarubai is responsible for the villagers’ health. The women visit pregnant women and give medicine to some of the older people. Today they are visiting their first patient. Rani Kale doesn’t come from Jawalke. She lives about an hour away, but her village doesn’t have anyone like Sarubai to help mothers-to-be. Sarubai is checking Rani and she is worried about the position of the baby. Rani might need to go to the hospital.

Half an hour later, Sarubai and Babai visit another mother with a three-month-old baby. While they are checking the baby, Sarubai gives the mother advice on healthy eating and vaccinations. Jawalke is a very different place because of the two women. They regularly deliver babies and continue to help as the child grows up. There is a shortage of doctors in this region, so village health workers are important because they can give medicine and advice.

A mobile team visits Jawalke once a week. The team includes a nurse and a doctor. The mobile team meets with Sarubai, and they look at any of her patients with serious medical problems. The health workers are an important connection between the mobile team and the local people. Currently there are 300 village health workers in the region, and the number is growing.
How often does Sarubai visit people in the village?

once a day
twice a day
twice a week
Sarubai Salve visits people in the village twice a day.

Mobile medicine

The village has about 240 families and, with another woman named Babai Sathe, Sarubai is responsible for the villagers’ health. The women visit pregnant women and give medicine to some of the older people. Today they are visiting their first patient. Rani Kale doesn’t come from Jawalke. She lives about an hour away, but her village doesn’t have anyone like Sarubai to help mothers-to-be. Sarubai is checking Rani and she is worried about the position of the baby. Rani might need to go to the hospital.

Half an hour later, Sarubai and Babai visit another mother with a three-month-old baby. While they are checking the baby, Sarubai gives the mother advice on healthy eating and vaccinations. Jawalke is a very different place because of the two women. They regularly deliver babies and continue to help as the child grows up. There is a shortage of doctors in this region, so village health workers are important because they can give medicine and advice.

A mobile team visits Jawalke once a week. The team includes a nurse and a doctor. The mobile team meets with Sarubai, and they look at any of her patients with serious medical problems. The health workers are an important connection between the mobile team and the local people. Currently there are 300 village health workers in the region, and the number is growing.
How many doctors are there in the village of Jawalke?

one
two
none
There are no doctors in the village of Jawalke.

Mobile medicine

Sarubai Salve goes to work twice a day. She leaves her home once at 9:00 a.m. and then again at 6:00 p.m. to visit people in her village of Jawalke. The village has about 240 families and, with another woman named Babai Sathe, Sarubai is responsible for the villagers’ health. The women visit pregnant women and give medicine to some of the older people. Today they are visiting their first patient. Rani Kale doesn’t come from Jawalke. She lives about an hour away, but her village doesn’t have anyone like Sarubai to help mothers-to-be. Sarubai is checking Rani and she is worried about the position of the baby. Rani might need to go to the hospital.

Half an hour later, Sarubai and Babai visit another mother with a three-month-old baby. While they are checking the baby, Sarubai gives the mother advice on healthy eating and vaccinations. Jawalke is a very different place because of the two women. They regularly deliver babies and continue to help as the child grows up. There is a shortage of doctors in this region, so village health workers are important because they can give medicine and advice.

A mobile team visits Jawalke once a week. The team includes a nurse and a doctor. The mobile team meets with Sarubai, and they look at any of her patients with serious medical problems. The health workers are an important connection between the mobile team and the local people. Currently there are 300 village health workers in the region, and the number is growing.
Where does Rani Kale come from?

Jawalke
a village near Jawalke
we don't know

Sarubai meets Rani because she is                  .

a new mother
pregnant
sick

Which of these statements is true about the health workers?

They only deliver babies.
They do the same job as doctors.
They have many different responsibilities.

What is the purpose of the mobile team?

to do the job of the health workers
to provide more medical help
to train the health workers

How do we know from the article that the village health project is successful?

because they are training more health workers
because patients say they are happy with their health workers
because the region doesn't need any more doctors

Where does Rani Kale come from?
- A village near Jawalke.

Sarubai meets Rani because she is 
- Pregnant.

Which of these statements is true about the health workers?
- They have many different responsibilities.

What is the purpose of the mobile team?
- To provide more medical help.

How do we know from the article that the village health project is successful?
- Because they are training more health workers.

looks after (verb phrase): 
2. when a woman is going to have a baby (adj): 
3. a person with a medical problem who sees a doctor (n): 
4. women who are going to have a baby (n): 
5. a place for people with medical problems (n): 
6. help a woman have a baby (v): 
7. not enough of something (n): 
8. can move from place to place (adj): 
9. important and sometimes dangerous (adj): 
10. near or in the same area (adj):

1. looks after (verb phrase): takes care of
2. when a woman is going to have a baby (adj): pregnant
3. a person with a medical problem who sees a doctor (n): patient
4. women who are going to have a baby (n): expectant mothers
5. a place for people with medical problems (n): medical facility/clinic/hospital
6. help a woman have a baby (v): assist in childbirth
7. not enough of something (n): shortage
8. can move from place to place (adj): mobile
9. important and sometimes dangerous (adj): crucial/risky
10. near or in the same area (adj): nearby/in the vicinity

feel

Write six different questions with the word feel. Use these words. You can use words more than once. When you are finished, click on the Feedback button to see possible answers. 
what
like
OK
cold
how do you
playing a game
doing something

1. What does it feel like playing a game?
2. How do you feel when doing something?
3. What do you feel when it's cold?
4. How do you feel about doing something?
5. What does it feel like when you're OK?
6. How do you feel when it's cold outside?
